page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-90 is a compute-optimized virtual machine powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor using the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 45 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The instance is configured with 177.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 1.97 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, and provides zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in multi-core processing and specialized instruction execution. The server achieves top 10% scores in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, Passmark CPU extended instructions, and Passmark CPU encryption tests, supported by hardware-accelerated AVX-512 and cryptography flags. It also demonstrates strong performance in software compilation via Clang, ray tracing, and static web serving throughput. Memory read bandwidth is strong at up to 215.31 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, though large-block write bandwidth and single-core decompression tasks fall into the average tier.

The c3d-highcpu-90 offers high compute density, making it a qualitatively cost-efficient choice for highly parallelized, CPU-bound workloads that do not demand massive memory allocations. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs represents a tradeoff, focusing resources entirely on raw x86 execution. This profile is highly suited for software compilation pipelines, cryptography, ray tracing, high-throughput web serving, and prompt processing for small to medium language models.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-90 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor. It delivers 90 dedicated vCPUs across 45 physical cores, paired with 177.0 GB of memory, resulting in a lean 1.97 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance does not feature bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark data highlights top-tier multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, and encryption capabilities, alongside strong memory read bandwidth. This resource distribution makes the server highly cost-efficient for parallelized, CPU-intensive tasks rather than memory-heavy operations. It is well-suited for workloads such as software compilation, cryptography, ray tracing, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highcpu-90 is a compute-optimized server with 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 177 GB RAM, delivering top-tier multi-core performance, software compilation, and cryptography execution.

tagline: A high-density, compute-optimized virtual server featuring 90 AMD EPYC vCPUs for demanding multi-threaded processing, cryptography, and compilation workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processor with 90 dedicated vCPUs and 45 physical cores.
Equipped with 177.0 GB of RAM, optimized for compute-heavy workloads.
Top-tier performance in multi-core execution, software compilation, and cryptography.
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, maximizing CPU resource density.
Ideal for parallel software builds, ray tracing, and high-throughput web serving.
